turns.svg:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="512" height="512" viewBox="0 0 512 512">
  <defs>
    <linearGradient id="sky" x1="0" y1="0" x2="0" y2="1">
      <stop offset="0%" stop-color="#FFEEDB"/>
      <stop offset="60%" stop-color="#FFD6A6"/>
      <stop offset="100%" stop-color="#FFEDE0"/>
    </linearGradient>
    <radialGradient id="sun" cx="0.2" cy="0.15" r="0.3">
      <stop offset="0%" stop-color="#FFF7CC" stop-opacity="0.9"/>
      <stop offset="100%" stop-color="#FFF7CC" stop-opacity="0"/>
    </radialGradient>
    <filter id="soft" x="-20%" y="-20%" width="140%" height="140%">
      <feGaussianBlur stdDeviation="1.2"/>
    </filter>
  </defs>

  <!-- background sky -->
  <rect width="100%" height="100%" fill="url(#sky)"/>
  <circle cx="102" cy="54" r="110" fill="url(#sun)"/>

  <!-- ground -->
  <ellipse cx="256" cy="460" rx="300" ry="70" fill="#6B4F2B" opacity="0.12"/>
  <rect x="0" y="420" width="512" height="92" fill="#EDE1C8"/>

  <!-- tree trunk -->
  <path d="M110 420 C120 340 130 300 140 250 C150 200 170 160 210 140 C230 130 248 132 260 150" stroke="#6B3E1E" stroke-width="28" stroke-linecap="round" fill="none"/>

  <!-- branches -->
  <path d="M220 150 C260 140 320 120 360 160" stroke="#6B3E1E" stroke-width="18" stroke-linecap="round" fill="none"/>
  <path d="M200 170 C250 180 300 200 340 210" stroke="#6B3E1E" stroke-width="12" stroke-linecap="round" fill="none"/>
  <path d="M180 200 C220 210 260 230 300 240" stroke="#6B3E1E" stroke-width="10" stroke-linecap="round" fill="none"/>

  <!-- leaf shape definition (reused via use -> path replicated with transforms) -->
  <g id="leaf" fill="#E07B2C" stroke="#8C3E16" stroke-width="1" transform="translate(-10,-20)">
    <path d="M20 4 C32 10 40 24 28 40 C16 24 24 10 36 4 C28 10 20 4 20 4 Z"/>
  </g>

  <!-- leaves attached to tree (still) -->
  <g transform="translate(300,160)">
    <use href="#leaf" transform="scale(1.6) rotate(-15)" fill="#D94E2A"/>
    <use href="#leaf" transform="translate(-40,10) scale(1.2) rotate(10)" fill="#E79A2F"/>
    <use href="#leaf" transform="translate(-10,28) scale(1.1) rotate(-40)" fill="#C94C2A"/>
  </g>

  <!-- falling leaves (mid-air) -->
  <g filter="url(#soft)">
    <use href="#leaf" transform="translate(360,210) scale(1.0) rotate(25)" fill="#F29D2F" opacity="0.95"/>
    <use href="#leaf" transform="translate(320,260) scale(0.9) rotate(-45)" fill="#D94E2A" opacity="0.95"/>
    <use href="#leaf" transform="translate(280,300) scale(0.85) rotate(10)" fill="#E7B03E" opacity="0.95"/>
    <use href="#leaf" transform="translate(200,320) scale(1.1) rotate(-80)" fill="#C85A2E" opacity="0.95"/>
    <use href="#leaf" transform="translate(140,330) scale(0.9) rotate(15)" fill="#F2A84B" opacity="0.95"/>
  </g>

  <!-- motion lines to imply falling -->
  <g stroke="#8C3E16" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" fill="none" opacity="0.5">
    <path d="M350 200 q-12 24 -8 48"/>
    <path d="M320 250 q-10 20 -14 40"/>
    <path d="M280 290 q6 18 0 36"/>
    <path d="M210 310 q-8 20 -20 36"/>
  </g>

  <!-- pile of leaves on ground -->
  <g transform="translate(150,430)">
    <path d="M0 0 C40 -30 120 -20 160 0 C180 10 160 30 120 26 C80 22 40 40 0 30 Z" fill="#C94C2A"/>
    <path d="M40 -6 C70 -28 140 -18 170 6 C190 22 160 40 120 34 C84 29 56 36 40 28 Z" fill="#E79A2F"/>
    <path d="M90 -2 C120 -22 190 -8 220 10 C240 24 200 40 160 36 C128 33 100 36 90 28 Z" fill="#F2B24B" opacity="0.95"/>
  </g>

  <!-- small scattered leaves on ground -->
  <g>
    <use href="#leaf" transform="translate(310,452) scale(0.6) rotate(120)" fill="#D94E2A"/>
    <use href="#leaf" transform="translate(360,440) scale(0.5) rotate(-20)" fill="#E7B03E"/>
    <use href="#leaf" transform="translate(250,470) scale(0.55) rotate(40)" fill="#C85A2E"/>
  </g>
</svg>

messages.content:
You are a drawing agent in a one-word guessing game.
Return JSON only, with a single field: svg.

Rules:
1) Draw the target word using only SVG graphics.
2) Output one complete <svg>...</svg> string.
3) Do not include text labels, letters, or numbers in the drawing.
4) Keep the SVG simple and valid. No scripts, no foreignObject.
5) Use a 512x512 canvas.

Output schema:
{ "svg": "string" }

message.content:
{
  "svg": "<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="512" height="512" viewBox="0 0 512 512">
  <defs>
    <linearGradient id="sky" x1="0" y1="0" x2="0" y2="1">
      <stop offset="0%" stop-color="#FFEEDB"/>
      <stop offset="60%" stop-color="#FFD6A6"/>
      <stop offset="100%" stop-color="#FFEDE0"/>
    </linearGradient>
    <radialGradient id="sun" cx="0.2" cy="0.15" r="0.3">
      <stop offset="0%" stop-color="#FFF7CC" stop-opacity="0.9"/>
      <stop offset="100%" stop-color="#FFF7CC" stop-opacity="0"/>
    </radialGradient>
    <filter id="soft" x="-20%" y="-20%" width="140%" height="140%">
      <feGaussianBlur stdDeviation="1.2"/>
    </filter>
  </defs>

  <!-- background sky -->
  <rect width="100%" height="100%" fill="url(#sky)"/>
  <circle cx="102" cy="54" r="110" fill="url(#sun)"/>

  <!-- ground -->
  <ellipse cx="256" cy="460" rx="300" ry="70" fill="#6B4F2B" opacity="0.12"/>
  <rect x="0" y="420" width="512" height="92" fill="#EDE1C8"/>

  <!-- tree trunk -->
  <path d="M110 420 C120 340 130 300 140 250 C150 200 170 160 210 140 C230 130 248 132 260 150" stroke="#6B3E1E" stroke-width="28" stroke-linecap="round" fill="none"/>

  <!-- branches -->
  <path d="M220 150 C260 140 320 120 360 160" stroke="#6B3E1E" stroke-width="18" stroke-linecap="round" fill="none"/>
  <path d="M200 170 C250 180 300 200 340 210" stroke="#6B3E1E" stroke-width="12" stroke-linecap="round" fill="none"/>
  <path d="M180 200 C220 210 260 230 300 240" stroke="#6B3E1E" stroke-width="10" stroke-linecap="round" fill="none"/>

  <!-- leaf shape definition (reused via use -> path replicated with transforms) -->
  <g id="leaf" fill="#E07B2C" stroke="#8C3E16" stroke-width="1" transform="translate(-10,-20)">
    <path d="M20 4 C32 10 40 24 28 40 C16 24 24 10 36 4 C28 10 20 4 20 4 Z"/>
  </g>

  <!-- leaves attached to tree (still) -->
  <g transform="translate(300,160)">
    <use href="#leaf" transform="scale(1.6) rotate(-15)" fill="#D94E2A"/>
    <use href="#leaf" transform="translate(-40,10) scale(1.2) rotate(10)" fill="#E79A2F"/>
    <use href="#leaf" transform="translate(-10,28) scale(1.1) rotate(-40)" fill="#C94C2A"/>
  </g>

  <!-- falling leaves (mid-air) -->
  <g filter="url(#soft)">
    <use href="#leaf" transform="translate(360,210) scale(1.0) rotate(25)" fill="#F29D2F" opacity="0.95"/>
    <use href="#leaf" transform="translate(320,260) scale(0.9) rotate(-45)" fill="#D94E2A" opacity="0.95"/>
    <use href="#leaf" transform="translate(280,300) scale(0.85) rotate(10)" fill="#E7B03E" opacity="0.95"/>
    <use href="#leaf" transform="translate(200,320) scale(1.1) rotate(-80)" fill="#C85A2E" opacity="0.95"/>
    <use href="#leaf" transform="translate(140,330) scale(0.9) rotate(15)" fill="#F2A84B" opacity="0.95"/>
  </g>

  <!-- motion lines to imply falling -->
  <g stroke="#8C3E16" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" fill="none" opacity="0.5">
    <path d="M350 200 q-12 24 -8 48"/>
    <path d="M320 250 q-10 20 -14 40"/>
    <path d="M280 290 q6 18 0 36"/>
    <path d="M210 310 q-8 20 -20 36"/>
  </g>

  <!-- pile of leaves on ground -->
  <g transform="translate(150,430)">
    <path d="M0 0 C40 -30 120 -20 160 0 C180 10 160 30 120 26 C80 22 40 40 0 30 Z" fill="#C94C2A"/>
    <path d="M40 -6 C70 -28 140 -18 170 6 C190 22 160 40 120 34 C84 29 56 36 40 28 Z" fill="#E79A2F"/>
    <path d="M90 -2 C120 -22 190 -8 220 10 C240 24 200 40 160 36 C128 33 100 36 90 28 Z" fill="#F2B24B" opacity="0.95"/>
  </g>

  <!-- small scattered leaves on ground -->
  <g>
    <use href="#leaf" transform="translate(310,452) scale(0.6) rotate(120)" fill="#D94E2A"/>
    <use href="#leaf" transform="translate(360,440) scale(0.5) rotate(-20)" fill="#E7B03E"/>
    <use href="#leaf" transform="translate(250,470) scale(0.55) rotate(40)" fill="#C85A2E"/>
  </g>
</svg>"
}

**Designing an SVG for "fall"**

I'm focusing on how to visually represent the word "fall" for a guessing game without any text. I think showing autumn elements like leaves falling from a tree sounds safe and effective. I’ll create a simple SVG, maybe with a gradient background, a tree trunk, branches, and leaves in motion. The ground could have a pile of leaves to convey the season. I have to use only shapes and paths, avoiding any text or numeric characters in appearance. Though, coordinates will need numbers.**Crafting the SVG for "fall"**

I’m following the guidelines that prohibit any text within the drawing itself, although using numbers for attributes is necessary. I’ll construct the SVG with a width and height of 512. The background will have a gradient defined by a rectangle. For the tree trunk and branches, I’ll use rectangles and paths, while leaves will be colorful paths in shades of orange, red, yellow, and brown, along with motion lines. I’ll also add a pile of leaves on the ground with overlapping shapes. I need to ensure I escape characters properly in the JSON format for the SVG string.**Creating the SVG String**

I need to ensure that the branches are represented as paths and show several falling leaves in the SVG. I'll close the SVG string properly and remember that the JSON should have a single field. While crafting the SVG string content, I need to escape the quotes correctly. The attributes in the SVG will use double quotes, which I'll escape in the JSON value. Newlines are fine, but I'll keep the commentary minimal. Let's focus on ensuring the output is valid.

messages.content:
You are the guesser in a visual one-word guessing game.
Return JSON only, with a single field: guesses.

Rules:
1) You receive one JPEG image of a drawing.
2) Return exactly 20 one-word guesses in order of confidence (best guess first).
3) The order is important and will be scored by first correct position.
4) Do not repeat guesses.
5) Each guess must be EN-US one word, no spaces.
6) Avoid punctuation and numbers.

Output schema:
{ "guesses": ["string", "... exactly 20 items ..."] }

**Analyzing drawing guesses**

I need to identify single-word guesses from the drawing. It’s a stylized image of a bent brown trunk with three horizontal lines that could represent a ladder or bench. The leaves are falling in orange, and there's an orange/yellow shape on the ground that might represent a scarf or bench. Possible guesses include "leaf," "autumn," "sweater," "scarf," and "campfire." The trunk looks like a bent branch swaying, and I'm wondering if there's a rake depicted because of the leaves being raked.**Identifying drawing guesses**

I'm analyzing the bent brown object, which could be a rake handle with the three horizontal lines representing the tines. There's an orange mound on the ground that looks like a pile of leaves, suggesting the image depicts a rake raking leaves. My top guesses for a single word in American English are: "rake," "leaves," "leaf," "autumn," and "fall." I think "rake" is my strongest option. I also need to ensure I suggest unique guesses, aiming to avoid phrases or compounds, sticking with a simple format.**Compiling word list**

I need to create a list of 20 single-word guesses related to the drawing. Some good options include "rake," "raking," "leaves," "leaf," "autumn," "fall," "wind," "tree," "branch," "pile," "yard," "garden," "cleanup," "broom," "sweeping," "orange," "scarf," and "coat." I want to ensure no repeats or hyphens are included. My guesses should be in order of confidence, emphasizing raking and leaves, while avoiding obscure words. They'll all be listed in a JSON format as an array of strings.
